How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-13 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 CONFLUENCE flags tickers appearing on 2+ screeners within one trading day, with the edge coming from validated cross-screener agreement filtering noise to high-signal overlap.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUM leads at a score of 1791, hitting CLENOW_MOMENTUM, CS_RANKER, and PULLBACK - the momentum-plus-relative-strength-plus-dip template the screener's validated combos favor most. PANW is second at 1008 on CLENOW_MOMENTUM, CS_RANKER, and VOLUME_LEADER, swapping the pullback tag for a volume confirmation and making it the cleaner breakout candidate. MS clears four screeners at 180, including VARIANCE_PREMIUM, the only name in the batch with a meaningful options signal attached. The score distribution is sharply top-heavy: HUM and PANW are outliers separated by a wide gap from a cluster of eight names packed between 97 and 188. None of the picks matched a validated combo, which strips out the highest-confidence overlay (WR 81.7%, Sharpe 2.68), and a NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 adds no directional tailwind, so HUM is the first choice at medium conviction for a pullback entry within a confirmed uptrend.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: AON (score 4, target no v2 estimate).

0 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta direction, filtered by EMA8/21 trend alignment and low-OI anomaly.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1QQQ ↑50 Watch$721.10 +1.31%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking has just 5 resolved trades out of 295 total, making the reported 100% win rate and 200% average return statistically meaningless as a confirmation of the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe - the key number is 5, not 100, and the monthly cohorts' consistent 100% WR across April through June carries the same caveat until resolved counts scale up.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 295 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin to the point of being actionless. The only signal is QQQ at score 50, Watch tier, flagged on a bullish sweep, but score 50 sits at the absolute floor of what this screener surfaces and zero names cleared 65. Score distribution is a single point with no dispersion, which is not a batch, it is noise. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.44, adding no directional conviction to an already marginal name. Pass on QQQ today and revisit only if a second sweep prints with a score above 65.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the backtest: Signal-tier names ran an 82% win rate at +4.2% avg versus a -0.4% SPY baseline over 90 days, and monthly cohorts improved from 51% WR in April to 84% in June, consistent with the 2.34 backtest Sharpe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DUK, PCG, and EXC all print 76 at the top of the board, all utilities, which is the sector Sinclair's table rewards most heavily at a +31% premium bias. The batch is tightly compressed: all 36 signals land between 65 and 76, median 69, nothing clears 80, so today is a structural sector play rather than a clean outlier call. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 neither amplifies nor suppresses variance-premium trades, making the utilities cluster the dominant factor. CPB at 75 is the only non-utility in the 75-plus band if sector concentration is a concern. Start with DUK at moderate conviction - the utility sector bias is baked into construction and 76 is the ceiling for today's batch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

TEVA at score 100 and IONQ at 99 are the standout names, both Very strong tier, with AA at 97 a close third. Pattern data is absent for all three, so the edge rests purely on VRP positioning rather than any structural setup. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 names score above 80 and 22 above 65, with a median of 75, meaning the batch skews quality rather than thin. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 offers no directional overlay and neither helps nor hurts, leaving VRP selection as the sole driver today. Lead with TEVA on its maximum score at moderate conviction.

🧠 Flags stocks where put volume exceeds 3x the 30-day average while price holds, reading institutional hedging in calm tape as contrarian upside.
Construction
Put volume > 3× avg(30d) + price stable or up. Kerberos thesis: contrarian - institutional hedging activity in calm tape often precedes upside continuation. Score weighs put vol ratio, price stability, IV crush.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPut volPut ratioPut premium
1FISV ↑100●●● Very strong$49.99 -2.33%455817.23$6029K

4SANM ↑65● Signal$207.40 +2.77%16093.26$2506K
5MGM ↑60 Watch$46.79 -0.95%39683.04$1150K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

THC hits 100 (Very Strong, LONG) and is the only name clearing the 80 threshold; GIS comes in at 70 (Signal, LONG), which is the screener's effective floor. Neither name carries a named pattern, so conviction rests entirely on the vol/price/IV composite score. Two signals with a median of 70 makes this a thin, top-heavy batch - not a clustered setup day. Macro NEUTRAL at 54.44 is a non-event for a contrarian thesis that does not require directional regime support. THC first at high conviction; treat GIS as a watch candidate only.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking is uninformative - zero resolved trades out of 620 open, well short of the 20 needed to score - so the 65% backtest win rate and 1.10 Sharpe are unconfirmed, but the monthly cohort sequence is the key data point: Feb's 24.6% win rate and -3.3% average followed by March's 67.3% and +9.8% and June's 62.4% and +2.86% shows the screener is highly regime-sensitive and currently in recovery, which broadly supports rather than contradicts the backtest.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 55% · avg +2.6% · beats SPY +1.4% by +1.2 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 55% · avg +2.6% · vs SPY +1.4%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 498 closed · WR TB 40% · 23% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MGM and SG both score 100 with a LAUNCH pattern, making them the headline names, but the more interesting signal is the trucking cluster: KNX, LSTR, TFII, and ARCB all score 95 and fire together, which reads as sector-level rotation rather than individual stock noise. Score distribution is unusually compressed - all 50 signals sit at or above 80 with a median of 85 and zero below 65 - meaning the filter is tight but the intra-batch spread is narrow, offering little ranking edge between names. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.44, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for pullback reversals, so the statistical edge exists but the environmental runway is shorter than in a confirmed bull-tier regime. Take MGM first at moderate conviction given the perfect score and clean pattern; treat the trucking cluster as a secondary package only if you want explicit sector exposure rather than a single name.

Four names share the 94-score ceiling today - IDCC, NNE, PPTA, and SMR, all Very strong tier - with IE and LAC following at 91, and the score distribution confirming how concentrated this batch is: 19 of 20 signals print at or above 80, median at 84, almost no low-conviction filler. First preference is the four-name 94-score cluster sized at 1-2% per name via LEAPS as the construction specifies, at moderate conviction, with no basis in today's data for concentrating in one name over another.

🧠 Combines VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to pinpoint vol-of-vol climax moments and flag sell-vol entry conditions.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑64 Watch$751.90 +0.36%SELL VOL1.64
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape posts 68% WR and avg +1.5% on 28 resolved trades for a KEEP verdict, but the alpha over SPY is effectively zero at +0.0 percentage points, the Signal tier (7 trades, 85.7% WR, +2.73% avg) is carrying the entire edge while Watch names (21 trades, 61.9% WR, +1.09% avg) barely keep pace with the index, and monthly cohort averages compressing from +5.43% in April to +0.53% in June confirm the near-term tape is softening - a trend that partially contradicts the VALID backtest label given all core backtest stats (Sharpe, n-trades, win rate) are null and rigorous is flagged false.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Catches options volume spikes above 5x the 20-day mean on calm, non-extended stocks to surface institutional positioning ahead of price moves.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Only 16 of 620 signals have resolved, making the 100% win rate and 194.7% average return versus SPY's 0.11% statistically empty - the WATCH verdict exists precisely because n=16 confirms nothing, and the backtest's 61.1% win rate at a p_bonferroni of 0.097 remains the only real validity anchor, marginal by any standard.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLI is the only name above 80, scoring 92 at Very strong, flagged on a BEARISH SWEEP but tagged LONG - heavy put flow on the industrials ETF is the signal, and the screener treats that as a fade or positioning setup worth watching. CRDO at 66 offers the cleanest directional read in the batch, a BULLISH SWEEP where flow and direction agree on a semiconductor name. FISV also hits 66 but repeats the BEARISH SWEEP/LONG conflict, dulling the read. The score distribution is thin at the top: median sits at 57, only 1 name above 80 and 3 above 65, with five names clustered in Watch territory, making this a narrow batch rather than a broad signal day. NEUTRAL macro at 54.44 neither accelerates nor kills the thesis. Lead with CRDO for directional clarity, XLI second if the put-fade is your frame, both at measured conviction.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLE and XLC are the only names worth discussing, both scoring 95 (Very strong), though neither carries a tagged pattern, leaving the entry catalyst undefined. XLF at 65 is the lone Signal-tier name, sitting between the two clusters but barely above the pack. The score distribution is starkly bifurcated: 2 names at the maximum of 95, one at 65, and five names pinned at the median of 55, with no gradation in between. The macro regime at NEUTRAL, 54.44, offers no directional tailwind and does not confirm a sector-rotation setup. First preference is XLE over XLC on the basis that energy more typically leads in late-cycle reflationary tape, but with zero pattern confirmation and a MARGINAL backtest behind this screen, sizing should be minimal.

🧠 Catches capitulation-volume reversal candles on stocks down 40%-plus from their 52-week high, betting on a near-term mean-reversion bounce.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape confirms the backtest: a 62.5% WR nearly matches the 63.3% backtest figure and the 6.88% average return well exceeds the 3.55% backtest mean, though monthly cohorts reveal the edge collapsed in January-February 2026 (29% WR, -12.9% average in January), flagging real regime sensitivity that the NEUTRAL current backdrop does not fully dismiss.

HON scores a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier with a breakout pattern and is the clear lead name today. ABR, FUTU, and IE follow at 91, all Very strong breakouts, offering a solid second tranche. The Strong-tier names (GFI, HTZ, LI, RGC at 87) carry a live-data warning: Strong-tier signals ran only 36% WR at -2.8% average over the past 90 days, well below the Signal tier's 9.2% average, so those four should be skipped or sized down sharply. Score distribution is adequate but not exceptional - all 50 signals clear the 65 floor, only 17 breach 80, and the median sits at 74. Neutral macro (54.44) offers no regime tailwind, so lead with HON at full conviction and treat ABR, FUTU, IE as secondary half-size entries.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on 90-day regression slope times R-squared, filtering for trend position, gap limits, and SPY above MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM is the only name worth discussing today, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very Strong tier while every other pick sits in Watch, with PANW a distant second at 56 and AMAT, CSCO, UNH, ELV, CVS, and CAT clustered between 10 and 28. The score distribution is as thin as it gets: 18 signals, median 9, one name above 65, one above 80 - this is not a broad momentum tape, it is one outlier surrounded by noise. The managed care cluster (HUM, UNH, ELV, CVS) is a sector tell, but only HUM clears the screener's own quality threshold and UNH through CVS are Watch-tier at best. Macro regime reads NEUTRAL at 54.44, meaning the SPY gate is open but the broader tape offers no directional tailwind. Take HUM first and only HUM, at moderate conviction - the Watch names do not belong in a momentum book under current conditions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%

min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more intermarket pairs breach their 7-day 2-sigma correlation band, betting on mean reversion across cross-asset relationships.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Captures institutional re-rating after beats: strong EPS surprise plus a held gap signals conviction buying that historically extends over 1-3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$11.36 +6.02%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape (WR 56.3%, avg +3.79%, beats SPY by +3.4 pts, verdict KEEP) broadly confirms the screener has live edge, but the June cohort's 36.5% WR and -0.30% avg is the key number that warns the drift has stalled in the most recent resolved month, contradicting the otherwise supportive backtest-level stats.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KEEP verdict, with an 80% win rate at 20 days and avg +5.6% beating SPY by 3.5 points across all three tiers, but the May 2026 cohort's collapse to 50.9% WR and -0.6% avg shows this screener bleeds in choppy tape, making June's recovery to 71.4% WR the key number that justifies acting on today's batch.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is completely ceiling-pinned: all 50 signals score 100, median 100, with zero differentiation within the batch, which makes rank-ordering arbitrary. Among the named picks, COP and BP are the highest-liquidity names with direct energy-sector weight, while BN (Brookfield) offers a financial and asset-management angle. BCD, BNT, CPSH, CMRE, and DIBS are thinner names with no pattern data attached, which limits conviction on any of them individually. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 provides no directional tailwind, consistent with a tape that neither rewards nor punishes momentum cleanly right now. Take COP first at moderate conviction given its liquidity, sector relevance, and the ability to exit cleanly if the energy bid fades.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape supports the KEEP verdict at 63.5% WR and avg +6.5% vs SPY +2.3%, but the critical contradiction is that the Very strong tier, 15 names at the top of the score range, posted just 33% WR and avg -2.1%, meaning the live edge sits in the mid-tier Signal and Watch buckets, not the highest scores.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ANAB tops the batch at 95 but the 90d data shows the Very strong tier posts just 33% WR and avg -2.1%, making the highest score a trap, not a green light. ABEO, ABX, ACHC, and BV cluster at 85 in the Strong tier, which carries a cleaner 61% WR and +4.0% avg in live tracking and is the more reliable landing zone. The score distribution is broadly top-loaded: all 50 signals cleared the 65 floor, 11 reached 80 or above, and the median sits at 70, meaning the screener is firing wide today rather than selectively. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.44, removing any regime amplifier for squeeze setups. Among the 85-tier names, prefer ACHC or BV first, at low-to-moderate conviction given the neutral tape and the diffuse rather than concentrated score distribution.

🧠 Flags Russell 1000 stocks where relative volume times multi-horizon price momentum ranks highest, surfacing potential trend leaders on rising institutional participation.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MRNA, PSX, SOFI, and XENE tie at a perfect 100 score with ANET and META at 99, but all 50 signals score at least 80 with a median of 96, making the distribution top-heavy and effectively undiscriminating across the entire batch. No pattern data is populated for any name, removing one layer of confirmation from every candidate. The neutral macro regime at 54.44 neither blocks nor amplifies momentum trades today. META at 99 and ANET at 99 carry more liquid options markets than the smaller names sharing the 100 score, which at least allows tighter defined-risk expressions if a position is warranted. This is a weak batch on differentiation - ANET is the first look at low conviction only, and only with sized-down exposure.

🧠 Flags large OTM options blocks (>$200K, 1-10 DTE) on quiet names, confirmed by next-day OI delta to distinguish new accumulation from closing trades.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape contradicts any optimism from the observational backtest: 417 resolved trades produce a mean return of -1.7% versus SPY at +1.0%, the Strong tier logs a 36% win rate with avg -7.7% (the worst performing tier, not the best), and monthly cohorts confirm deterioration as avg returns fell from +8.4% in April to +0.7% in June, earning the KILL verdict.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects rejection-tail bars at least 1.8x ATR at daily value-area extremes via Market Profile, flagging excess reversals where mean-reversion backtested positive only under strict location filters.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape (6 resolved, 66.67% WR, +1.73% avg vs SPY's +0.13%) directionally supports the filtered backtest's +3.18% at 10 days, but 6 resolved trades is the key number - it sits far below the 20-trade minimum for the WATCH verdict to carry any statistical weight, and the June 2026 cohort's average dipped to -0.40% despite a 66.67% WR, a dispersion warning the sample is too small to dismiss.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, RPHS, scoring 90 and sitting in the 'Very strong' tier with a LONG direction, though the pattern field is null so the specific tail structure is unverifiable from the data. Score distribution is trivially concentrated - single signal, max and median both 90, one name above 80, nothing else in the queue. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for a mean-reversion setup, which is roughly the correct environment for excess-reversal plays. Take RPHS first, but size conservatively: one-name batches with a missing pattern tag and a structurally thin backtest (N=74, no block bootstrap) do not warrant full conviction.

One signal today: KNCT scores a perfect 100, tier Very Strong, direction SHORT, with no pattern tag populated. If trading this batch, KNCT is the only name available, and the absence of a pattern tag leaves the setup leaning on score alone - size it small and treat it as a probe rather than a conviction trade.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Catches post-earnings continuation entries by waiting for a red weekly pullback within 4 weeks of an earnings gap, then buying the weekly reclaim.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%

Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

BBIO scores 100 and sits in the Very strong tier, direction LONG, making it the only name the screener surfaced today, with no pattern data to add texture. 🧠 SMART_FLOW signals stripped of any setup with KVO or Force Index crosses within 3 days, lifting 60-day paper win rate from 46% to 67%.
Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AGL is the only name scoring above 65, hitting the ceiling at 100 in the Very strong tier, though pattern data is null across every pick, which strips away setup-level conviction. HYMC at 36 and DLLL at 31 both land in Watch and are not actionable. The score distribution is severely top-heavy - median of 13 with 29 of 30 signals below 65 - this batch is thin by any measure. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 neither blocks nor sponsors momentum setups, so the tape is not the reason to skip today. AGL first if you must act, but do it at low conviction: the Very strong tier in live tracking is the single worst-performing tier at -5.9% average 20-day return, which argues for sizing small until the tape clears.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CRNX and DFTX both score 80 and hit TRIGGER, the strongest pattern in the batch, but the Strong tier has exactly one resolved live trade in 90-day tracking and that trade lost 34% against the short. ATEX, NRIX, and RPD score 75 in SOW ZONE and are the next tier down. With 23 of 50 signals at 65 or above and a median of 60, the distribution is modestly top-heavy but not decisive. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 offers no directional help for a hedge screener built on exhaustion reversals. Given the backtest rejection and the single Strong-tier live disaster, CRNX is the first name to watch if forced to pick one, but conviction is minimal - size it small or skip the batch entirely.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Flags deeply oversold equities via weekly RSI(14) below 30, then scores daily reversal candlesticks and zone positioning for contrarian long entries.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $CAPR $AEM $OR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern
1CAPR ↑70● Signal$20.66 +0.24%13.0229.80NOHAMMER
2AEM ↑65● Signal$145.74 +1.55%34.5025.65YESNONE
3OR ↑60 Watch$29.05 +1.13%30.3328.56YESHAMMER
4RYAM ↑60 Watch$7.21 +1.48%30.6927.67YESNONE
5DQ ↑58 Watch$11.86 +1.98%30.0617.95YESNONE
6JKS ↑58 Watch$15.66 +2.69%33.2722.37YESNONE
7AMR ↑55 Watch$154.56 +3.94%31.9623.96YESNONE
8NAUT ↑53 Watch$1.80 +1.40%27.5022.74NOHAMMER
9HON ↑50 Watch$222.87 +0.28%7.9022.09NONONE
10TDS ↑50 Watch$34.03 +1.92%17.4218.84NONONE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape (WR 56.6%, avg +5.2% on 424 resolved trades vs. SPY +0.83%) confirms a durable edge the doctrine-rejected backtest could not validate, but the monthly cohort trend shows WR compressing from 78.8% in March to 54.1% in June with May avg going negative at -0.44%, so the 4.4-point SPY spread is the load-bearing number here, not the win rate.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DKL is the only name worth leading with - it scores 66, clears the Signal tier, and is the sole pick above 65 in a 9-signal batch. BORR at 63 and FANG at 61 both carry SETUP status in Watch, which keeps them relevant, but the gap down to DKL is the most meaningful separation this batch offers. Score distribution is tight and uninspiring: max 66, median 59, zero names clearing 80, which is a compressed read by any standard for this screener. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (54.44), providing no directional tailwind for a contrarian reversal play, and the batch skews heavily toward energy names across DKL, BORR, FANG, CRGY, XOM, and EGY, layering sector concentration on top of an already thin score spread. Take DKL first at reduced size given the weak batch quality, and park everything at 61 or below on the watchlist until a setup pattern firms.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven mega-session 2026-05-16 crypto battery: 0/16 signals survive adversarial validation post-spot-ETF 2024 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects directional pressure in crypto-linked ETFs by scoring volume-vs-mean spikes, premium deviations, and OI delta weighted against spot crypto correlation.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $COIN $FBTC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1COIN ↑55 Watch$159.19 +1.16%44.840.4%risk_on
2FBTC ↑50 Watch$56.36 +4.12%44.030.2%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape contradicts any remaining edge: a 39.7% win rate with avg return of -5.5% against SPY's +0.25% is a -5.8 point gap, and the June 2026 cohort (0% win rate, avg -17.6% across 41 trades) shows the signal is deteriorating sharply, not mean-reverting toward the one-trade April outlier.

IDNA and ARKG both score 70 and hold Signal tier, making them the top reads in a batch that maxes out at 70 with zero names clearing 80. XBI at 66 adds another biotech vehicle to the front of the list, but no name carries a pattern flag, which strips setup definition from every candidate. With 50 signals and only 4 above 65, the distribution is compressed and the median of 57 confirms most of the batch is noise. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.44 provides no directional tailwind, so this is not a conviction environment. If forced to act, IDNA first on a small position at low conviction, with ARKG a close second given identical scores.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch delivers eight perfect-100 Very strong signals but pattern data is null across all of them, so the scoring rationale is a black box. BL and CELH are the two cleaner equity names at max score and the first to evaluate. SRTY, a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF, flagged LONG at 100, is the most operationally important name to investigate - it either reflects intentional small-cap bearishness embedded in the score or a screener failure to exclude inverse instruments. Score distribution is broadly populated above 65 (41 of 50 signals) with a right-tail cluster of eight at 100 and median 70, not unusually concentrated but top-heavy in today's batch. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.44, no directional tailwind, so first preference is BL at half size until the SRTY anomaly is resolved.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is fully ceiling-ed: all 50 signals land at 100, median 100, which strips any within-batch differentiation from the model itself. META and ASML headline the top picks as the names most likely to represent new conviction adds across multiple whales, while MKSI is the least-followed name in the set, where concentrated accumulation carries a cleaner signal than consensus mega-caps. NEUTRAL macro at 54.44 does not help a broad 13F basket, and the 60-to-90-day staleness in the underlying filings compounds that drag in a tape that has moved since print. Prioritize MKSI at moderate conviction as the non-obvious name; treat META and ASML as confirmatory reads on existing theses, not fresh screener-driven entries.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FINS and COE lead on perfect scores of 100, with SVRE one point behind at 99, and all three carry a Very strong tier with no accompanying pattern data, leaving the insider rank-times-value sort as the only discriminant. The score distribution is top-heavy to an extreme: all 48 signals clear 80, the median sits at 94, and the 8-name top-picks list spans only 4 points from 96 to 100, meaning the absolute score is nearly meaningless for within-batch ranking. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.44, removing any directional tailwind for the signal. Start with FINS at low conviction on the perfect score, but keep size modest in the absence of both pattern confirmation and regime support.

EOD
Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks non-financial stocks by EV/EBIT ascending to surface the cheapest operating businesses, betting that extreme cheapness mean-reverts to fair value.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Neither the live tape (0 of 69 signals resolved, wr_20d null, verdict WATCH) nor the empty monthly cohort table can confirm or contradict a backtest that does not yet exist - the only number available is a 56.7% win rate on 30 tracking-basket positions with 0% expired, which is uninformative.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ leads at score 100 (Very strong), making it the most statistically cheap name in today's 16-signal batch by the screener's own ranking. WRD at 97 and KROS at 93 round out a trio of Very strong signals with no pattern confirmation attached to any of them. Score distribution is top-heavy but not thin: 7 of 16 names print at or above 80 and the median sits at 73, so the batch has real density at the quality end. Macro reads NEUTRAL at 54.44, which provides no style tailwind for deep value today and no headwind either. Take BZ first on maximum score, but hold conviction low until the backtest battery closes - this screener has zero validated edge on record.

🧠 Flags stocks where 3 or more insiders file Form 4 cluster buys within 30 days, weighted by C-suite rank, dollar value, and recency.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No formal backtest exists yet, so live tracking is the only evidence on hand - the 90-day tape's 63% win rate and avg +5.7% against SPY's -0.5%, sustained across May and June cohorts at 61-64% WR, makes the KEEP verdict credible but entirely unvalidated by rigorous historical work.

🧠 Finds stocks lagging their LATE_CHASE or RIDING sector, scoring catch-up potential via sector relative strength times stock under-performance.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength within bullish sectors, combining sector breadth and cluster ranking to surface rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$11.13 +1.23%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$145.22 +0.21%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$25.34 -0.63%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$136.03 +0.04%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$3.92 -0.38%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$80.08 +1.61%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (57% WR, +4.4% avg, +6.1 points above SPY's -1.7%) and both monthly cohorts trending positive confirm a live edge with no formal backtest to compare against, with the Signal tier's 73% WR being the key number that drives the overall result.

AAMI (76, Signal) and ABCL (74, Signal) are the names to focus on - the Signal tier carries a 73% win rate and a +9.5% average 20-day return in the 90-day live track, making tier assignment more predictive than raw score here. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects parabolic price, call-volume spikes, and Reddit surges with no earnings catalyst, flagging crowded retail longs primed for mean reversion.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AMBA $BE $ADTN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AMBA ↓81●● Strong$72.87 +1.21%EUPHORIA0.81-3.8%
2BE ↓79● Signal$245.66 +5.21%EUPHORIA0.79-1.0%
3ADTN ↓79● Signal$12.72 +2.33%EUPHORIA0.79-1.4%
4AAP ↓73● Signal$54.51 -1.45%GREED0.731.3%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No completed backtest exists to measure against - N=17 has not cleared the 30-trade minimum - but the 90-day live tape issues its own verdict with the time-based win rate of 31.7% doing the most damage, meaning options positions are expiring worthless nearly seven times in ten even as the directional win rate prints a misleading 57%, and June's cohort of 41 signals averaged -1.64% versus May's +1.79%, a deteriorating month-over-month trend that, combined with the KILL verdict, gives this screener no live edge to trade against today.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 TRAP flags bear traps when price closes below value area low then reclaims it next day, squeezing trapped shorts into a long entry.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ASC $AGCO $ARCC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ASC ↑65● Signal$16.22 +2.46%BEAR_TRAP15.2518.600.83
2AGCO ↑60 Watch$113.61 -0.41%BEAR_TRAP115.01117.723.75
3ARCC ↑60 Watch$18.72 +0.43%BEAR_TRAP18.3618.680.33
4AS ↑55 Watch$34.29 +0.41%BEAR_TRAP33.4336.351.25
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 There are no backtest stats to compare against (N=0, not yet backtested), but the 90d live record is the only data point: 66.7% win rate across 54 resolved trades, avg +5.1% versus SPY's +0.1%, with monthly cohorts at 76.9% in May fading to 63.4% in June, confirming the verdict holds but momentum is softening.

🧠 TRAP_SHORT flags fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside, trapping longs and generating a short entry signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ALNY $ARQT $ARWR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20, targeting mispricings in implied vol before the catalyst.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $UPS $INTC $NEE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1UPS ↑65● Signal$112.54 -0.31%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2INTC ↑55 Watch$106.93 +3.69%PRE_IV_RAMP9—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3NEE ↑55 Watch$89.55 +1.32%PRE_IV_RAMP10—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE is the lone signal today at score 55, tier Watch, and that tells you the batch is weak before you look further. One name sitting exactly at the median with zero signals clearing 65 and zero clearing 80 is not a tradeable set, it is a near-empty screen. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.44) neither supports nor suppresses a volatility long thesis, and NEE carries no pattern data to sharpen the case. Pass on the batch entirely; if a position is required, NEE long vol is a watch-only placeholder at minimal size until the screen fills out.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags 25-delta put/call skew spikes above the 90-day baseline and takes the contrarian trade when the underlying is simultaneously calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR $SBUX $TSLA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑61 Watch$97.40 +5.75%66%990.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2SBUX ↓55 Watch$107.39 +0.05%41%770.09SELL PUT (put skew rich)
3TSLA ↑53 Watch$397.82 +0.78%38%45—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the overall edge at 56% WR and +7.4% avg vs SPY +1.3%, but tier performance inverts badly: Very Strong and Strong score 30% and 25% WR at roughly -4% average each while Watch tier leads at 68% WR and +13.7% avg, a reversal that calls the score model's calibration into question, and June's avg falling to -1.2% despite a 59% WR adds a further caution on recent return compression.

🧠 Detects curated smart-money options flow by layering unusual volume and directional signals against insider and congressional activity, stripping mechanical hedges.
Construction
Post-screener curation: combines UNUSUAL_V3 + SMART_FLOW + BTFD + BOTTOM signals with insider/congress overlay. Conviction score 0-10, exclusion of gamma hedging and ETF hedges.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies stocks where put IV swamps call IV above the 90th percentile, flagging outsized hedging flow as a contrarian long setup when bears become overcrowded.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$395.45 +2.97%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).

🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 spikes above 1.5 standard deviations on a rolling 90-day window, identifying elevated fear premium ripe for options sellers.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $MA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1MA ↓85●● Strong$540.13 +0.45%54%25%2.13EXTREME_FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Composite of IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and flow scoring that hunts mispricings between implied and realized volatility.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.44 -1.56%55%847%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress risk using the Altman Z-score, a weighted five-ratio accounting model that predicts bankruptcy probability.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

🧠 STFR is a standard EOD screener; full construction logic lives in the source script and is not documented in this payload.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional accumulation or distribution by comparing price spread to volume, identifying effort-versus-result divergences.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Pass on the VSA list entirely.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
